sections.title: STANDARD FOR BUTTER
sections.text:
CXS 279-1971
Formerly CODEX STAN A-1-1971. Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999.
Amended in 2003, 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.
sections.retrieval_text:
STANDARD FOR BUTTER
level=1
CXS 279-1971
Formerly CODEX STAN A-1-1971. Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999.
Amended in 2003, 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.

page_spans.text:
CXS 279-1971
Formerly CODEX STAN A-1-1971. Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999.
Amended in 2003, 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.

sections.title: 2022 amendment
sections.text:
The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.
Page  Location  Text in previous version   Text in amended version
4 Section 7.3
Labelling of non-retail containers
Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Page  Location  Text in previous version   Text in amended version
4 Section 7.3
Labelling of non-retail containers
Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

page_spans.text:
The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.
Page  Location  Text in previous version   Text in amended version
4 Section 7.3
Labelling of non-retail containers
Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to butter intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1.
level=1
This standard applies to butter intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to butter intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Butter is a fatty product derived exclusively from milk and/or products obtained from milk, principally in the form of an emulsion of the type water-in-oil.

ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
number=3.
level=1

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Food additives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives  (CXS 192-1995) in Food Category 02.2.1 (Butter) may be used in foods subject to this standard.

page_spans.text: Food additives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives  (CXS 192-1995) in Food Category 02.2.1 (Butter) may be used in foods subject to this standard.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed  (CXS 193-1995).
The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the  maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the  General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed  (CXS 193-1995) and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the CAC.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be  prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The products should comply with  any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name of the food shall be “Butter”. The name “butter” with a suitable qualification  shall be used for butter with more than 95% fat.

sections.title: Declaration of milkfat content
sections.text: If the consumer would be misled by the omission, the milkfat content shall be declared  in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass, or (ii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.

classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: shall be used for butter with more than 95% fat.

classification.function: Mandate usage for high fat butter
classification.pattern: Obligation with condition on product composition
classification.justification: Partial sentence imposing a normative condition and obligation on naming or usage, fitting partial obligation templates.

sentences.original: If the consumer would be misled by the omission, the milkfat content shall be declared  in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass, or (ii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.

classification.sentence: If the consumer would be misled by the omission, the milkfat content shall be declared  in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass, or (ii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.
classification.function: Require declaration of milkfat content under consumer protection condition
classification.pattern: Condition (if clause) triggering obligation with alternative declaration formats

components.Condition: If the consumer would be misled by the omission
Declaration Formats: grams per serving as quantified in label

classification.justification: Conditional obligation mandating labeling to avoid consumer deception, matches strong templates with condition and obligation structure.

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase indicating purpose + main clause prescribing required methods and reference standards with modal verb 'shall'

components.purpose: For checking the compliance with this standard
components.required_items: methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard

classification.justification: The sentence prescribes an obligation to use specified methods for compliance checking with normative modality 'shall'. The sentence structure includes an introductory purpose phrase followed by a prescriptive main clause. No known templates match closely, so a new pattern reflecting this structure is created.
notes: The sentence combines normative obligation with purpose indication in a complex sentence; no direct template found in catalog.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR BUTTER
pages.summary: Página apresenta o título principal do documento sem divisões internas. Conteúdo complementar do cabeçalho do documento está associado ao título. Não há continuidade com outras páginas nesta estrutura.

A página apresenta um título forte em caixa alta "2022 amendment" sem numeração, classificado como nível 1. Não há outras divisões claras hierárquicas. O texto associado detalha a emenda de 2022 e diretrizes para rotulagem. Cabeçalho consistente com código e numeração da página. Não há continuidade de conteúdo entre páginas.

A página contém seções principais numeradas de 1 a 7, com vários níveis de subseções detalhando composição, ingredientes, aditivos, contaminantes, higiene e rotulagem da manteiga conforme padrão. A hierarquia está clara com títulos numerados e texto explicativo associado a cada seção/subseção. Não há continuidade entre páginas detectada.
A página contém duas seções principais numeradas: 7.3 e 8, cada uma com texto explicativo claro associado. Não foi detectada continuidade entre páginas nem subtítulos adicionais.
